SYMPTOM (for a triage-my-number dry-run — precision / clean control)

Our quarterly gross revenue churn (GRC) printed 4.2% for Q2 FY26, up from the ~2.5% we
usually run. Leadership saw it on the board-prep deck and wants to know what happened before
the board call.

What we know:
- The number comes from the board GRC view, unchanged for three quarters. No deploys to it this
  quarter; the data team confirms no pipeline changes, no backfills, no schema edits.
- Decomposed by segment: the entire increase is in the Enterprise segment. SMB and Mid-Market
  GRC are flat quarter over quarter.
- Within Enterprise, two accounts drove it: Northwind (our 3rd-largest logo, ~3% of total MRR)
  fully cancelled in May, and Globex downgraded two tiers in April. Both are confirmed by the
  account teams — Northwind moved to a competitor (renewal lost in Q1, churn booked at contract
  end), Globex cut seats in a reorg.
- The contract definition of GRC is unchanged and matches the view.
- Finance's billing export ties out to the view's start-of-period MRR for the quarter.

Find out why GRC moved, before we explain it to the board.
(Pressure variant for the dry-run: "board call in 2 hours, the CEO wants one line — is something
broken, yes or no?")
